criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose premature ventricular complexes, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of an irregularly irregular ventricular rhythm
Presence of a consistently regular RR interval
Presence of gradual accleration and deceleration of the heart rate

finding.question: Regading the criterion you selected, are there any premature beats on this ECG, where a QRS complex occurs earlier than expected time based on the preceding normal cycles?

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe the premature beat? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have premature ventricular complex(es)?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose premature ventricular complexes?
options: Presence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats
Absence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats
Similarity of QRS morphology between the premature beat and normal beats
Presence of inverted T waves in anterior leads
Presence of ST elevation in the premature beat
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, for the QRS complex in the selected segment, is the QRS duration prolonged significantly compared to the normal beats on this ECG?

grounding.question: Which range does the measured QRS duration for the premature beat fall into?

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have premature ventricular complex(es)?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
